title: Scanning optical probe with flexing mechanism
abstract: A scanning optical probe includes a cannula, optical fiber, lens, and an actuating mechanism for tilting the optical fiber back and forth within the cannula. An actuator in the probe handle is coupled to various flexing and guide components extending through the cannula and towards the distal end of the scanning optical probe. Reciprocating motion from the actuator is transmitted to the components thereby causing the optical fiber at the distal end of the scanning optical probe to aim across target surfaces. The light emitted from the optical fiber is processed to generate a scan of the target area.
claims: Claims CLAIMS We Claim: 1. A scanning optical probe comprising: a cannula comprising a proximal section, distal section, and a lumen extending there through; an optical fiber extending through the lumen and into the distal section of the cannula; a lens disposed in the distal section of the lumen, and distal to the optical fiber; an elongate flexible member disposed within the lumen of the cannula, and comprising a proximal region, an end region, and a passageway through which the optical fiber extends; and an actuator coupled to the elongate flexible member at the proximal region, and adapted to provide reciprocating motion to the elongate flexible member, wherein the end region of the elongate flexible member deflects between a first position and a second position responsive to the reciprocating motion such that light emitted from the optical fiber is directed in a first direction and a second direction corresponding respectively to the first position and the second position of the end region of the elongate flexible member. 2. The scanning optical probe of claim 1 wherein the elongate flexible member comprises a tubular body, and a central axis extending there through. 3. The scanning optical probe of claim 2 wherein the elongate flexible member further comprises a face disposed in a perpendicular orientation to the central axis of the tubular body, and wherein the optical fiber is attached to the face. 4. The scanning optical probe of claim 3 wherein the elongate flexible member further comprises a first arm extending from the body to the face, and a second arm opposite the first arm and extending from the face to the body. 5. The scanning optical probe of claim 4 wherein a proximal portion of the first arm is fixed to the cannula, and relative axial movement between cannula and elongate flexible member deflects the end region of the elongate flexible member. 6. The scanning optical probe of claim 5 wherein the face comprises an aperture through which the optical fiber is attached. 7. The scanning optical probe of claim 6 wherein the first arm, second arm, and face of the flexible elongate member are integrally formed of one material. 8. The scanning optical probe of claim 2 wherein the end region of the elongate flexible member comprises a first predefined articulation segment. 9. The scanning optical probe of claim 8 wherein the first predefined articulation segment comprises a set of slots formed in a side wall of the elongate flexible member. 10. The scanning optical probe of claim 9 wherein the elongate flexible member further comprises an elongate first rod extending proximally from the first predefined articulation segment, and wherein said first rod is axially moveable relative to the tubular body of the elongate flexible member such that axial movement of the first rod causes the end region of the elongate flexible member to deflect at the first predefined articulation segment. 11. The scanning optical probe of claim 10 wherein each of the slots has a teardrop shape. 12. The scanning optical probe of claim 11 wherein the lens is fixed to the optical fiber. 13. The scanning optical probe of claim 1 further comprising a handle wherein said cannula extends distally from the handle. 14. The scanning optical probe of claim 13 wherein the actuator is located within the handle. 15. The scanning optical probe of claim 14 wherein the lens is fixed within the lumen of the cannula.
